original_theorem: (a) Union of open sets is open. (b) Intersection of closed sets is closed.
theorem_translation:
```lean4

    IsOpen (‚čÉ‚āÄ S‚āĀ) ‚ąß IsClosed (‚čā‚āÄ S‚āā) := by
```

import Mathlib
import Aesop

set_option maxHeartbeats 0
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat Filter
theorem union_of_open_is_open_and_intersection_of_closed_is_closed
    {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X]
    (S‚āĀ : Set (Set X)) (h‚āĀ : ‚ąÄ s ‚ąą S‚āĀ, IsOpen s)
    (S‚āā : Set (Set X)) (h‚āā : ‚ąÄ s ‚ąą S‚āā, IsClosed s) :
    IsOpen (‚čÉ‚āÄ S‚āĀ) ‚ąß IsClosed (‚čā‚āÄ S‚āā) := by sorry

proof_steps.natural_language:
We assume:
‚ÄĘ Let G be a collection of open sets in a metric space (X, d) [tc_1].
‚ÄĘ Let S be the union of the sets in G, i.e., S = ‚ą™_{g ‚ąą G} g [def_1].
‚ÄĘ Let x‚āÄ be an arbitrary point in S.
Therefore, we conclude:
‚ÄĘ There exists an őĶ > 0 such that the open ball B(x‚āÄ, őĶ) is a subset of S [l1].
proof_steps.lean_tactics:
constructor
  ¬∑ exact isOpen_sUnion h‚āĀ
  ¬∑ rw [isClosed_iff_isOpen_compl, Set.compl_sInter]
    apply isOpen_sUnion
    rintro _ ‚ü®s, hs, rfl‚ü©
    exact (h‚āā s hs).isOpen_compl
